title: Tractor-Trailer Injury in Mobile, Alabama: Legal Steps (2026)

Tractor-trailer crashes in Mobile, Alabama present unique legal challenges that go far beyond a typical car accident — and the stakes for injured victims are enormous.

Here's what most people don't realize: critical evidence like electronic logging device (ELD) data, black-box recordings, and driver inspection reports can be overwritten or legally destroyed within days of a crash. Once it's gone, it's gone.

Alabama's 2-year statute of limitations may sound like plenty of time, but trucking companies and their insurers deploy rapid-response legal teams immediately after a serious collision. Every hour you wait is an hour they use to build their defense.

At WreckMatch, our network of 800+ experienced law firms across the country includes attorneys who specialize specifically in commercial trucking litigation — lawyers who know how to demand spoliation holds, subpoena carrier records, and negotiate against billion-dollar freight companies.

If you or someone you know was injured by a tractor-trailer in Mobile or anywhere in Alabama, the smartest first step is getting matched with the right attorney today — not tomorrow.

If a tractor-trailer hit you or a loved one in Mobile, Alabama, the next few days are the most important of your entire case.

Trucking companies move fast. Their insurance adjusters and legal teams are often on the phone within hours of a crash — protecting the company's interests, not yours. Black-box data, dashcam footage, and driver logs can disappear quickly if no one acts to preserve them.

Alabama gives you 2 years to file a claim, but waiting even a few weeks can cost you the evidence that wins cases.

reddit_title: Hit by a tractor-trailer in Alabama — what legal steps should you take in the first 48 hours?
reddit_body:
Tractor-trailer accidents are fundamentally different from regular car crashes, and most people don't realize how quickly the legal window starts closing. In Alabama, the statute of limitations is 2 years, but the real deadline is often much sooner — commercial trucks carry electronic logging devices (ELDs) and black boxes that record speed, braking, and hours of service data. Trucking companies are not always required to preserve this data indefinitely, so getting a legal hold request out fast is critical.

The first thing you should do after seeking medical attention is avoid speaking to the trucking company's insurance adjuster without legal representation. These adjusters are trained to minimize payouts and may ask you leading questions or offer a quick settlement that doesn't come close to covering long-term costs. Document everything you can from the scene — photos, witness contacts, the truck's DOT number and carrier name on the door.

For anyone in this situation in Mobile or elsewhere in Alabama, connecting with an attorney who specifically handles commercial trucking cases makes a real difference. A general personal injury lawyer may not know how to subpoena carrier safety records or challenge hours-of-service violations. Sites like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys experienced in truck accident litigation at no cost, which is a solid starting point if you don't already have a lawyer in mind.
